detail.query_output: def CLIPTextEncodeSDXL(clip: CLIP, width: INT = ..., height: INT = ..., crop_w: INT = ..., crop_h: INT = ..., target_width: INT = ..., target_height: INT = ..., text_g: STRING, text_l: STRING) -> CONDITIONING:

response.message: We need to switch to SDXL: update the checkpoint, text encoders, and latent dimensions. I'll search for `CLIPTextEncodeSDXL` to see its exact signature before making edits.

batch_result.batch:
checkpointloadersimple.ckpt_name = 'sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors'
emptylatentimage.height = 1024
emptylatentimage.width = 1024
positive_sdxl = CLIPTextEncodeSDXL(clip=checkpointloadersimple.clip, width=1024, height=1024, crop_w=0, crop_h=0, target_width=1024, target_height=1024, text_g='masterpiece, best quality', text_l='masterpiece, best quality', near=positive)
negative_sdxl = CLIPTextEncodeSDXL(clip=checkpointloadersimple.clip, width=1024, height=1024, crop_w=0, crop_h=0, target_width=1024, target_height=1024, text_g='lowres, bad anatomy, worst quality', text_l='lowres, bad anatomy, worst quality', near=negative)
ksampler.positive = positive_sdxl.CONDITIONING
ksampler.negative = negative_sdxl.CONDITIONING
ksampler_2.positive = positive_sdxl.CONDITIONING
ksampler_2.negative = negative_sdxl.CONDITIONING
del positive
del negative
done()

negative_sdxl = CLIPTextEncodeSDXL(clip=checkpointloadersimple.clip, width=1024, height=1024, crop_w=0, crop_h=0, target_width=1024, target_height=1024, text_g='lowres, bad anatomy, worst quality', text_l='lowres, bad anatomy, worst quality', near=negative)

ksampler_2.negative = negative_sdxl.CONDITIONING
